Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting chronic low blood glucose. Elevated creatinine and chloride, low CO₂, and abnormal urine findings (proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, brown color, ammoniacal odor) may indicate renal impairment and possible dehydration or metabolic disturbance. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function, metabolic status, and possible causes of hypoglycemia.
